response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test runners and assertion libraries — but the core required stack is essentially absent. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and C/C++; Java appears only peripherally, Kotlin and Spring not at all, and there is no evidence of deep JVM expertise or enterprise Java framework work, which are the non-negotiable requirements for this role.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus authored developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Long track record across web, desktop, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and active knowledge-sharing via workshops and talks
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, and only marginal Java exposure
No evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge required for JVM language tooling
Recent work is full-stack TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, far from enterprise JVM IDE subsystems
